verdict: FALSIFIED — does NOT clear the 80 wall. Stays at current tier.

primary_result_full_support.note: PGW == vanilla EXACTLY: with ~9000 training genes the STRING support covers the whole graph, so every held-out gene sits at distance 0 -> normalizer has zero dynamic range -> reduces to vanilla.

fair_result_sparse_support.setup: distance normalizer defined vs a 15% gene subsample so test genes sit 3-4 hops out (genuine range)
fair_result_sparse_support.PGW_vs_featureCP: featureCP BEATS PGW: paired mean +0.0120 in featCP favor, Wilcoxon p=0.009
fair_result_sparse_support.note: Even WITH graph-distance range, a plain isotonic sigma on log_baseMean (feature difficulty) calibrates better than the biology-graph distance normalizer.

prereg_falsifiers_fired: F1 (PGW does not beat LWCP): FIRED — PGW==vanilla, LWCP marginally better
F2 (graph sigma does not beat feature-space sigma): FIRED DECISIVELY — featureCP significantly better, p=0.009
honest_conclusion: The external biology graph (STRING) carries NO information about conformal prediction difficulty beyond what a single expression feature (log_baseMean) already provides, in this well-powered dataset. Two reasons: (1) at ~9000 perturbed genes the training support saturates the graph, killing distance range; (2) even with induced range, graph distance is a worse difficulty proxy than baseline expression. This is a genuine NEGATIVE RESULT, pre-registered and reported. It does NOT support a novel-primitive claim; the project correctly stays at its current calibration-benchmark tier (~77).
what_this_rules_out: A perturbation-graph-distance nonconformity normalizer is not the route past 80 on these datasets. Reported honestly rather than buried or tuned to pass.
citations_grounding_design: Roohani et al. 2024 Nat Biotech 42:927 (GEARS graph-connectivity failure mode)
